Operation.description: This operation finds the Mpr0BOPElement which was assigned with some Mdl0ModelElement (Cpd0DesignElement or Cpd0DesignFeature) but having different revisions.
Operation.name: findAffectedElements
Operation.returnDescription: This operation returns the list of Mpr0BOPElement which was assigned with some Mdl0ModelElement (Cpd0DesignElement or Cpd0DesignFeature) but having different revisions.
Operation.useCase: 1.    After the planner assigns some Cpd0DesignElement or Cpd0DesignFeature from the subset to Mpr0BOPElement, if the Cpd0DesignElement or Cpd0DesignFeature is revised and the product subset is re-calculated, planner needs to view the list of Mpr0BOPElement which was assigned with different revisions. When planner loads the BOP Element e.g. Mpr0MfgProcessElement or Mpr0ManufacturingElement, the product subset area shall display mismatch status against the Cpd0DesignElement or Cpd0DesignFeature. And from mismatch status when user click on "Find Whether Different Revision is Used" corresponding Mpr0BOPElement should get highlighted.

The operation supports the following types:
- A process element can be added as a predecessor of another process element, if both belong to the same activity.

dependency.

160059 The object type %1$ is not supported to be a successor.

ParameterInfo.description: List of cloned objects.
Represents action to be taken for clone objects when master is deleted.

Operation.description: Updates the clone objects from their masters. The supported types are Mpr0Activity, and Mpr0MfgProcessElement. Attribute Groups, Datasets, Model Views, Operations, flows, Assignments will be updated from the master.  In most cases, the result is that the target or cloned process/activity becomes identical to the master. However, some changes made to a cloned activity are preserved after an update, such as attribute groups, attachments, and operations,  and for a process - new operations, attribute groups, and flows between new operations.
Operation.name: updateCloneObjects
Operation.returnDescription:
Standard service data which may contain the following partial errors.
1. Assignments and model views were not mapped since the Collaborative Design objects are different.

ParameterInfo.description: List of objects to clone and target scope to paste the clones.
Revision Rule.
If true, the clone will be able to get updates from the master.

Operation.description: Clones the input objects in the given target scope with/without traceability. Supported types for cloning are Mpr0BuildElement, Mpr0Activity, and Mpr0MfgProcessElement. Cloning a process element clones its attribute groups, datasets, operations, flows, assignments and model views. Assignments and model views are cloned only if the target scope is in a different hull and the CD associated is same as the source. Cloning an activity clones its attribute groups, datasets along with its process elements. Cloning a build element involves cloning the sub build elements, activities and process elements.
Operation.name: cloneObjects
Operation.returnDescription:
This operation returns CloneObjectsResponse that contains cloned object information and the service data. Service data may contain the following partial errors.
1. Only Process type is supported.
2. Only Activity type is supported.
3. The given type is not a valid type of Build Element.
4. Assignments and model views were not mapped since the Collaborative Design objects are different.
5. The product subset associated with the source activity, was not copied to the new activity, since the Collaborative Design objects are different.
6. The Build Element cannot be cloned because it will result in cyclic dependency between parent Build Element and sub Build Element.
7. The Build Element cannot be pasted in itself.
8. The input is a clone of a master process and therefore cannot be pasted with traceability.
9. The processes are clones of master processes and therefore cannot be pasted with traceability.
10. The input is a clone of a master process and therefore cannot be pasted with traceability. Other processes were successfully cloned.
11. The input processes are clones of master processes and therefore cannot be pasted with traceability. Other processes were successfully cloned.

Operation.description: This operation create/updates a workset for input process element business object and returns it. Workset will contain foreground subset with the list of Cpd0DesignElement and Cpd0DesignFeature objects directly assigned to the process. Workset and foreground subset will have the same name and id as the input process. Workset also contains background subset for each predecessor of the process representing the incoming process assembly list. Background subset will have the same name as the incoming manufacturing element.

Output containing standard service data and Mpr0Disclosure object containing subset for direct assignment and subset for its predecessors. Service data may contain following errors:

- View Maker cannot be opened for input, because this process and all processes preceding it do not have assignment.

OperationTemplate.description: Clones the input objects in the given target scope with/without traceability. Supported types for cloning are Mpr0BuildElement, Mpr0Activity, and Mpr0MfgProcessElement. Cloning a process element clones its attribute groups, datasets, operations, flows, assignments and model views. Assignments and model views are cloned only if the target scope is in a different hull and the CD associated is same as the source. Cloning an activity clones its attribute groups, datasets along with its process elements. Cloning a build element involves cloning the sub build elements, activities and process elements.
OperationTemplate.name: Mpr0::Soa::Process::Clone::cloneObjects
OperationTemplate.returnDescription:
This operation returns CloneObjectsResponse that contains cloned object information and the service data. Service data may contain the following partial errors.
1. Only Process type is supported.
2. Only Activity type is supported.
3. The given type is not a valid type of Build Element.
4. Assignments and model views were not mapped since the Collaborative Design objects are different.
5. The product subset associated with the source activity, was not copied to the new activity, since the Collaborative Design objects are different.
6. The Build Element cannot be cloned because it will result in cyclic dependency between parent Build Element and sub Build Element.
7. The Build Element cannot be pasted in itself.
8. The input is a clone of a master process and therefore cannot be pasted with traceability.
9. The processes are clones of master processes and therefore cannot be pasted with traceability.
10. The input is a clone of a master process and therefore cannot be pasted with traceability. Other processes were successfully cloned.
11. The input processes are clones of master processes and therefore cannot be pasted with traceability. Other processes were successfully cloned.

This will contain values for the filter criteria.
Possible values for each filter are:

2. MismatchFilter - Mismatch
3. TypesToIncludeFilter - Cpd0DesignElement, Cpd0DesignFeature
4. TypesToExcludeFilter - Cpd0DesignElement, Cpd0DesignFeature
5. AssignmentTypeFilter - Mpr0AssignedParts, Mpr0AssignedFeatures
StructElement.name: filterValues

Struct.description:
The structure to pass information for various filters that may be applied on the product elements.
e.g. Cpd0DesignElement and Cpd0DesignFeature.
Valid values for filters that may be applied are AssignmentFilter, MismatchFilter, TypesToIncludeFilter, TypesToExcludeFilter, AssignmentTypeFilter.
Below is the description for each filter.

AssignmentFilter - This will filter in the product elements with the value for mpr0AssignmentCoverage attribute as Unassigned or Partially Assigned By Descendants.
MismatchFilter - This will filter in the product elements which are having updated revisions in the subset.
TypesToIncludeFilter - This will filter in the product elements which are instance of the given types.
TypesToExcludeFilter - This will filter out the product elements which are instance of the given types.
AssignmentTypeFilter - This will filter out the produuct elements based on assignment type.
Struct.name: ProductElementFilterCriteriaInput
